description: Changing a field's type or removing it inside a published version silently breaks clients that depend on the old shape.
body: Failure mode: a field is renamed, removed, or retyped within an existing API version to clean things up, and deployed clients break. Detection signals: clients error on deserialization after a deploy; generated client code no longer compiles against the contract; support reports a sudden surge of failures. Do not make non-additive changes inside a live version. Safer fallback: make the change additive with a new optional field, or introduce a new major version with a deprecation timeline, keeping the old shape until clients migrate.
